item.title: Supreme Court upholds mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
item.description: The Supreme Court has maintained mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone, setting aside for now a lower court order that blocked abortion providers from prescribing the widely used drug through telehealth and shipping it to patients.

item.title: Cuba considers U.S. offer of $100 million in aid amid power grid collapse
item.description: Cuba's national energy grid has suffered a major failure, cutting power to the island's eastern provinces.

item.title: South Carolina governor calls special session on redistricting
item.description: As Republicans seek to retain control of the U.S. House, leaders of both parties nationwide have sought to redraw their congressional maps to net more seats for their parties.

item.title: A Chinese community in New York City reacts to the Trump-Xi Beijing summit
item.description: There has been palpable excitement about President Trump's state visit to China in the Queens neighborhood of Flushing, home to one of the largest Asian populations in the U.S.

item.title: Seized ship taken toward Iran as Trump and Xi agree on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A ship was taken by unknown parties toward Iranian waters after an Indian-flagged vessel was attacked off Oman.

item.title: Xi draws red line on Taiwan at Trump summit
item.description: During their bilateral meeting on Thursday, Chinese President Xi Jinping warned President Trump of "conflicts" if Taiwan is not "handled properly." CBS News foreign correspondent Anna Coren has the latest from Beijing.

item.title: How the Trump-Xi summit could impact the U.S.-China AI race
item.description: President Trump's visit to Beijing comes as the U.S. and China compete for artificial intelligence supremacy. Matt Sheehan, senior fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, joins with analysis.

item.title: Looking back at Scoop the service dog's appearances on CBS News 24/7
item.description: CBS News 24/7 is saying goodbye to Scoop, a service dog in training for Canine Companions, as she heads to puppy college.

item.title: Supreme Court rules trucking broker can be sued after deadly crashes
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that a trucking broker at the center of a CBS News investigation can be sued following deadly crashes. CBS News chief investigative correspondent Jim Axelrod has the details.

item.title: What was said in OpenAI lawsuit closing arguments
item.description: Lawyers presented closing arguments Thursday in the OpenAI trial pitting Elon Musk against its CEO, Sam Altman. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more.

item.title: FBI offers reward for info on Air Force officer charged with spying for Iran
item.description: The FBI said Thursday that it's still trying to locate Monica Witt, who is accused of defecting to Iran in 2013 and revealing highly classified U.S. intelligence.

item.title: Video shows massive Russian aerial attack against Ukraine
item.description: Russia unleashed a massive two-day aerial barrage on Ukraine, the largest since the war began, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Thursday. These videos show the damage in the capital city, Kyiv.

item.title: Will Trump ask Putin to bring these Americans home?
item.description: Anastasia Antonov believes that her father, Aleksandr, was arrested by the Russian government last year because he is an American citizen. Now, she is appealing to President Trump to push Vladimir Putin's government to free her father.

item.title: House vote on Iran war powers resolution fails for third time
item.description: The vote on a Democratic resolution to rein in Mr. Trump's authority to strike Iran was 212-212, falling just short of a majority.

item.title: Rex Reed: A bold-faced-name life
item.description: The film critic and journalist talks about a life filled with movies, celebrities, and a penchant for speaking his mind

item.title: From the archives: Outspoken film critic Rex Reed
item.description: Film critic Rex Reed, whose clever and barbed opinions about movies – and movie stars – made him a fixture for decades in print and on television, died on May 12, 2026 at age 87. In this Feb. 4, 2018 "Sunday Morning" profile, Reed talked with Mo Rocca about how he came to live the life of an A-Lister himself. He also dispensed his unvarnished opinions about that year's best picture Oscar-nominees.

item.title: Why are so many U.S. CEOs in China with Trump, and what do they want?
item.description: More than a dozen American CEOs are accompanying President Trump on his trip to China. That's not unusual.

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will sue The New York Times
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government plans to file a defamation lawsuit against The New York Times.

item.title: Trucking broker can face lawsuit over fatal crash, Supreme Court rules
item.description: A CBS News investigation showed the broker had worked with dangerous "chameleon carriers," thousands of which evade federal safety enforcement by reincarnating under new names.

item.title: Watch: CENTCOM chief says Iran is "significantly degraded threat"
item.description: Admiral Brad Cooper, commander of the U.S. Central Command, told Sen. Jack Reed that Iran is a "significantly degraded threat" but the country still retains some capabilities. Cooper appeared before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Thursday.

item.title: Christopher Nolan | Sunday on 60 Minutes
item.description: Christopher Nolan, the filmmaker behind "Oppenheimer," "Interstellar," "The Dark Knight" and "The Odyssey," sits down with Scott Pelley. Sunday on 60 Minutes.

item.title: Software CEO convicted for running $1 billion Medicare fraud scheme
item.description: Brett Blackman was convicted on charges including healthcare and Medicare fraud, and faces decades in prison.

item.title: Trump Mobile says its T1 mobile phone to ship this week
item.description: Trump Mobile's $499 gold-toned phone has faced delays since it was unveiled in June 2025.

item.title: Bipartisan U.S.-Mexico congressional caucus launches amid diplomatic strain
item.description: The launch is being backed by the American Mexican Leadership Council, a new national organization also debuting Thursday to elevate Mexican American leadership and advance U.S.-Mexico collaboration.

item.title: 3 credit card debt questions borrowers should consider this week
item.description: Following a series of disappointing inflation reports, credit card users in debt should consider these questions now.

item.title: Former death row inmate granted bond after nearly being executed 3 times
item.description: An Oklahoma judge granted bond to former death row inmate Richard Glossip on Thursday, laying the groundwork for his first release from prison since 1997.

item.title: Senate adopts resolution to withhold senators' pay during shutdowns
item.description: The Senate unanimously agreed to adopt a resolution on Thursday that will withhold senators' pay during a lapse in funding for any federal agency.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 11 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Hero pilot recounts crash landing in Atlantic Ocean
item.description: Ian Nixon, a veteran pilot from the Bahamas, put the plane he was flying down in the ocean without anyone suffering serious injuries.

item.title: Rare blue-green diamond sells for record price at auction
item.description: The 5.5-carat "Ocean Dream" diamond was found in Central Africa in the 1990s.
